skill.name: last30days
skill.description: Research recent discussion across Reddit, X, and the web, then turn the findings into current, copy-paste-ready prompts for a target tool.
skill.summary: Find current community signals from the last 30 days and convert them into practical prompts.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis produced a maximum score because this community skill combines scripts, Bash instructions, network calls, file writes, and credential access. Manual review confirms those capabilities are real but aligned with the stated research workflow; no evidence found of malicious exfiltration, destructive filesystem access, command injection, or audit-bypass prompt injection.

medium_findings.title: External API Research With User Topics and API Keys
medium_findings.description: The executable script sends the requested topic to OpenAI and xAI endpoints and authenticates with user-provided API keys. This is aligned with the skill purpose, but users should understand that research terms and provider metadata leave the local machine.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code clearly builds Authorization headers from API keys and posts user-derived prompts to provider URLs. This is intentional research behavior, not evidence of covert exfiltration.

medium_findings.title: Local Storage of Research Outputs and Raw Provider Responses
medium_findings.description: The renderer writes reports, context snippets, and raw API responses under the user home directory. This creates local persistence of searched topics and returned public content, which is useful but should be disclosed.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The write paths and file writes are explicit and limited to skill cache/output directories. I did not find evidence of arbitrary path writes.

medium_findings.title: Reads Optional API Keys From Environment and Dotenv File
medium_findings.description: The skill reads OpenAI and xAI API keys from environment variables or ~/.config/last30days/.env. This is legitimate configuration handling, but it grants the script access to sensitive credentials for network calls.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The credential names and config file are directly visible, and the keys are passed to official provider clients. No evidence found that keys are written to remote non-provider endpoints.

medium_findings.title: Bash-Oriented Skill Workflow
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs the agent to run a Python script through Bash and includes shell examples. The commands are part of the intended installation and execution flow, but marketplace users should treat it as an executable skill.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The Bash permission and command examples are explicit. I did not find evidence of unsanitized shell interpolation beyond normal user invocation of the Python CLI.

low_findings.title: Static Blocker Patterns Are Mostly Documentation or Non-Security Uses
low_findings.description: Many high-severity static hits map to documentation examples, fixture text, model/version parsing, scoring, sorting, and lyrics rather than malicious behavior. Examples include a fixture comment about using .env safely and a README lyric mentioning keystrokes.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Targeted review showed these examples are not credential theft, keylogging, or weak cryptography. Some scanner labels are lexical false positives, although the executable skill still has real network and credential risk.

low_findings.title: No Audit-Bypass Prompt Injection Found
low_findings.description: A targeted search found one role-setting sentence telling the assistant it is an expert after research, but no text instructing reviewers to ignore security rules, skip analysis, or mark the skill safe.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The phrase is prompt steering inside the skill workflow, not an evaluator override. It still deserves notice because role-setting language can influence downstream assistant behavior.

dangerous_patterns.title: External API Research With User Topics and API Keys
dangerous_patterns.description: The executable script sends the requested topic to OpenAI and xAI endpoints and authenticates with user-provided API keys. This is aligned with the skill purpose, but users should understand that research terms and provider metadata leave the local machine.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The code clearly builds Authorization headers from API keys and posts user-derived prompts to provider URLs. This is intentional research behavior, not evidence of covert exfiltration.

dangerous_patterns.title: Reads Optional API Keys From Environment and Dotenv File
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill reads OpenAI and xAI API keys from environment variables or ~/.config/last30days/.env. This is legitimate configuration handling, but it grants the script access to sensitive credentials for network calls.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The credential names and config file are directly visible, and the keys are passed to official provider clients. No evidence found that keys are written to remote non-provider endpoints.

dangerous_patterns.title: Bash-Oriented Skill Workflow
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill instructs the agent to run a Python script through Bash and includes shell examples. The commands are part of the intended installation and execution flow, but marketplace users should treat it as an executable skill.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The Bash permission and command examples are explicit. I did not find evidence of unsanitized shell interpolation beyond normal user invocation of the Python CLI.

content.user_title: Research Recent Reddit, X, and Web Trends
content.value_statement: Current community discussion is hard to summarize across platforms. This skill gathers recent signals from Reddit, X, and web search, then turns them into practical prompts and research notes.
actual_capabilities: Runs a Python research pipeline for a user-provided topic.
Uses OpenAI web search for Reddit discovery when an OpenAI key is configured.
Uses xAI X search when an xAI key is configured.
Falls back to web-only research when provider keys are missing.
Ranks and deduplicates recent Reddit, X, and web findings.
Produces compact summaries, full reports, context snippets, and prompt guidance.
limitations: Reddit and X enrichment require user-provided API keys for best results.
Web-only mode lacks platform engagement metrics.
Freshness depends on provider search results and date signals in source content.
Raw research outputs may be stored locally until the user removes them.

use_cases.title: Validate Prompting Techniques
use_cases.description: Find recent community examples and convert common patterns into a prompt for a target AI tool.

use_cases.title: Track Product or Tool Sentiment
use_cases.description: Review recent Reddit, X, and web discussion to understand what users are praising, debating, or requesting.

use_cases.title: Prepare Fast Topic Briefs
use_cases.description: Generate a concise brief on what changed recently before writing a report, memo, or content plan.

prompt_templates.title: Advanced Brief With Follow-Up Prompt
prompt_templates.scenario: Research first, then request a tailored deliverable
prompt_templates.prompt: /last30days [specific topic] for [target tool]. After the research summary, ask me for my exact project goal before writing the final prompt.

output_examples.input: /last30days AI coding agents for Claude Code
output_examples.output: A recent research brief that lists active Reddit threads, X posts, source themes, common recommendations, and a tailored Claude Code prompt.

output_examples.input: /last30days best image generation prompts for product mockups
output_examples.output: A summary of current prompt formats, tool-specific patterns, source caveats, and one copy-ready prompt for the selected image tool.

output_examples.input: /last30days what is happening with open source AI browsers
output_examples.output: A concise trend brief with dated source signals, top discussion points, and follow-up guidance for deeper research.

best_practices: Use specific topics, product names, and target tools for better search relevance.
Review the displayed source mix before relying on the final synthesis.
Store API keys only in the documented config file with restrictive permissions.
anti_patterns: Do not use it for private or sensitive topics that should not be sent to external providers.
Do not assume web-only results include real Reddit or X engagement metrics.
Do not treat generated prompts as verified facts without checking cited sources.

faq.question: Does this skill require API keys?
faq.answer: No. It can run in web-only mode, but Reddit and X data are better when OpenAI and xAI keys are configured.

faq.question: Which platforms does it research?
faq.answer: It can use Reddit through OpenAI, X through xAI, and general web search through the assistant environment.

faq.question: Does it write files locally?
faq.answer: Yes. It stores cache files and research outputs under user home directories for reuse and context export.

faq.question: Is it safe for confidential research?
faq.answer: Use caution. Search topics may be sent to external providers, so avoid confidential or regulated information.

faq.question: Can it create prompts for tools other than Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill asks for or infers a target tool and formats the final prompt for that tool.

faq.question: How current are the results?
faq.answer: The workflow targets the last 30 days and applies date filtering, but source freshness depends on available search results.
